title: Coulteria glabra
intro: Coulteria glabra, also known as Coulter's cotton rose or Coulter's daisy, is a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in reducing pain and improving overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Coulteria glabra include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Coulteria glabra is often used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of colds and fever.
taxonomy: Coulteria glabra is a genus of flowering plants in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the tribe Senecioneae. Coulteria glabra is native to the southern Andes and is found in the mountainous regions of Chile and Argentina. The plant is classified as a dicot, having two cotyledons in its seedling stage. Coulteria glabra is further classified as a member of the subclass Asteridae within the class Magnoliopsida.
